pi_items.source: Web search on #800020 color name confirmed it is widely referred to as 'Burgundy' and can be called 'Deep Red' (https://www.color-hex.com/color/800020, https://www.colorhexa.com/800020)
HSV color analysis: #800020 converts to HSV H=345° which falls within deep red/burgundy range of 340-360 degrees. Confirmed by multiple color database sources.

Burgundy colors span across the red hue range wrapping around 0. Web search results showed burgundy wine shades can have hues around 2-5 degrees, confirming 15 is reasonable as an upper bound. HSV hue range 340-360 and 0-15 captures burgundy colors.

Web search explicitly confirmed DreamPlan uses .ddp file extension (https://www.file-extensions.org/ddp-file-extension-dreamplan-home-design-software-project). .ndp is associated with Nick-O-Matic Design Factory and digital pathology software, NOT DreamPlan.
Cannot verify - the export_result.ps1 script is missing from the repository. The task description references this filename, but implementation is incomplete.

Cannot verify - the export_result.ps1 script is missing from the repository. Path format is consistent with Windows paths used in other DreamPlan tasks (e.g., C:\Users\Docker\Desktop in complete_home_staging).

Unclear scale - metadata value 30 does not match verifier threshold of s > 50 (0-255 scale). If metadata uses 0-100 scale, 30 converts to ~76 in 0-255 scale. Purpose and scale of metadata value cannot be determined without additional context.

Unclear scale - metadata value 20 does not match verifier threshold of v > 40 (0-255 scale). If metadata uses 0-100 scale, 20 converts to ~51 in 0-255 scale. Purpose and scale of metadata value cannot be determined without additional context.

privileged_info_summary: The task targets the color #800020, which is authentically referred to as Burgundy or Deep Red. The hue range of 340-15 degrees is appropriate for capturing burgundy colors including the target #800020 (hue 345°). However, the expected project file extension is incorrectly specified as .ndp in the metadata and task description; DreamPlan Home Design Software uses .ddp extension for project files, not .ndp. The verifier checks file existence and screenshot content by reading from a JSON result file, but the export_result.ps1 script needed to generate this file is missing from the repository, making full verification impossible.
